content.text:
[cron:9babde66-dc85-47e9-9b6a-4f066acd031e PR监控] 检查 GitHub 上所有 open 的 PR，列出未处理的 PR 状态，给个简要汇报。
Current time: Wednesday, April 29th, 2026 - 20:02 (Asia/Shanghai) / 2026-04-29 12:02 UTC

Use the message tool if you need to notify the user directly with an explicit target. If you do not send directly, your final plain-text reply will be delivered automatically.

content.thinking:
The user wants me to check open PRs on GitHub and give a brief status report. Let me first figure out what repositories we should check. Since this is a personal workspace, let me search for relevant repos or check common patterns.

Actually, the cron trigger says "PR监控" (PR monitoring) - it's checking all open PRs on GitHub. I need to know which repos. Let me think about this... The user has a few likely repos based on their profile. But actually, they might want me to check specific repos. Since the cron doesn't specify a repo, I should probably look at what repositories exist under relevant orgs/usernames.
 
I'm not sure which repository or organization context applies here - it could be personal repos like alchain, or maybe work-related ones tied to this Feishu group chat. Let me start by checking the user's GitHub account for open PRs and see if that surfaces something meaningful. I'm realizing I don't have a clear list of repositories to check, so let me search across GitHub for any repos owned by alchain first—this should give me a good starting point even without knowing exactly which ones are active right now.

details.error:
MCP error -32603: Validation Error: Validation Failed
Details: {"message":"Validation Failed","errors":[{"message":"The listed users and repositories cannot be searched either because the resources do not exist or you do not have permission to view them.","resource":"Search","field":"q","code":"invalid"}],"documentation_url":"https://docs.github.com/v3/search/","status":"422"}

content.thinking:
The search didn't work. Let me try a different approach - maybe the username is different, or I need to search differently. Let me also check if there are any repos in the workspace that might be related to GitHub.

Actually, let me look at what's in the workspace directory structure first, and also try searching for common repo patterns.

arguments.query: GitHub repository open PR 监控
